name: sidecar
description: Battle-tested guardrails for Claude Code, ported from the hexa-native agent wilson.

plugins.name: wilson-guards
plugins.description: Three dancinlab-workflow PreToolUse guards in one bundle, standalone ports of wilson governance + guard-* predicates: ssot-lock (deny editing a file matched by an ssot-lock: bullet in the nearest AGENTS.md ## Governance), tape-append-only (a .log.tape event-history file is append-only — deny a rewriting Edit or an overwriting Write; per the tape v1.2 architecture-vs-history split a plain .tape is editable architecture, so the guard is inert for it), domain-lint (a root UPPERCASE.md topic roadmap must be Head + --- + ## Log). Each guard inert unless its convention is present. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GUARDS=1.

plugins.name: wilson-ssot
plugins.description: AGENTS.md walk-up SSOT injected at SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit (wilson agents-md plugin equivalent).

plugins.name: wilson-readme-format
plugins.description: Deny Write/Edit on a repo-root README.md that violates the readme-format anti-patterns (multi-glyph H1, non-English At-a-glance fence, forbidden #### heading). Standalone port of wilson's guard-readme-format — no wilson binary dependency. Ships a companion `docs-convention` skill teaching the README/UPPERCASE.md roadmap conventions up front — also covers wilson-guards/domain-lint.

plugins.name: wilson-prefs
plugins.description: User preferences (reply language / code language / response style) set via the /wilson-prefs:prefs slash command, persisted to plugin data, injected as context. SessionStart / PreCompact / PostCompact inject the full `## Prefs` block (PostCompact lands in the clean post-summary context — strictly stronger than PreCompact for surviving compaction); UserPromptSubmit re-asserts only the compact directive lines, EXCEPT every Nth turn (`refresh-every`, default 25) it re-injects the full body so the style stays fresh across a long session. Standalone port of wilson prefs — working. Injects nothing until the user sets a preference.

plugins.name: wilson-hexa-verify
plugins.description: PreToolUse: deny Bash invocations of non-hexa verifiers (sympy / PyPhi / wolframscript / mathematica / mathkernel) and redirect to the hexa CLI. PostToolUse: when `hexa verify` reports a new SUPPORTED equation, open a PR to dancinlab/hexa-lang baking the equation into the binary built-in atlas (fills `hexa atlas promote`'s stubbed pr step; PR left for human review, never auto-merged), falling back to prompting the worktree-pr workflow. Standalone port + extension of wilson guard-hexa-verify — working. INERT unless the `hexa` binary is on PATH.

plugins.name: wilson-dangerous-path
plugins.description: Deny Write/Edit/MultiEdit targeting a protected system path (/etc /usr /bin /sbin /System /.git /.gnupg) or a credential path (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, gh config, keychain, credentials). Standalone port of wilson guard-dangerous-path — working.

plugins.name: wilson-git-guard
plugins.description: Deny force-push at the PreToolUse layer — a Bash `git push` carrying --force / -f / a +refspec (and --force-with-lease, unless SIDECAR_ALLOW_FORCE_WITH_LEASE=1) is blocked. Standalone port of wilson's git-guard. Ships a companion `git-workflow` skill teaching the safe-push convention up front so the force-push deny rarely has to fire.

plugins.name: wilson-secret-guard
plugins.description: Block live secrets before they land. PreToolUse (Write/Edit/MultiEdit): deny writing a real .env file or any content carrying a high-confidence credential (AWS / GitHub / GitLab / Anthropic / OpenAI / Slack / Google / Stripe tokens, PEM private keys). UserPromptSubmit: block a prompt that pastes such a credential. High-confidence patterns only — near-zero false positives. Ships a companion `security` skill (wilson-sdlc-style) teaching the secret/path/destructive-shell convention up front so the deny rarely has to fire — also covers the sibling wilson-dangerous-path & wilson-bash-guard surface.

plugins.name: wilson-bash-guard
plugins.description: Deny catastrophic Bash commands at PreToolUse — pipe-to-shell from a network downloader (curl|sh), `rm -rf` of a root/home path, fork bombs, disk destroyers (dd of=/dev/disk, mkfs, >/dev/sd*), and recursive chmod/chown on / ~ or `.`. High-confidence destructive patterns only — near-zero false positives. Complements wilson-git-guard (force-push) and wilson-dangerous-path (protected-path writes). Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_BASH_GUARD=1.

plugins.name: wilson-output-trim
plugins.description: Token economy: PreToolUse rewrites Bash commands (updatedInput) so stdout passes a TF-IDF line-salience + MinHash near-dup filter before the model ingests it. Spirit-port of wilson compaction-prefilter — working. Small output passes verbatim; exit code preserved via pipefail.

plugins.name: wilson-pool
plugins.description: Route heavy Bash commands to a remote host roster via ssh (PreToolUse updatedInput) + /wilson-pool:pool config + SessionStart ## Pool block. Each host carries a platform tag (macOS-only / Linux-only commands go to a matching host, else round-robined) and an optional per-host workdir override. workdir may be explicit or `auto` (mirror the current project — local ~/<rel> → remote ~/<rel>). `autosync on` rsyncs the local project to the target host before each routed command — a continuously-changing tree stays fresh with zero manual sync, and the workdir is created if absent (additive by default; `autosync mirror` adds rsync --delete); with `autosync off` a pre-flight `test -d` check skips routing to any host lacking the workdir. Spirit-port of wilson's pool roster. Routing is OFF until the roster has >=1 host AND workdir is set; only Bash is routed.

plugins.name: wilson-checkpoint
plugins.description: Never lose work to a usage limit / rate-limit / crash. A CC hook cannot see the limit event, so this keeps an always-fresh non-destructive snapshot instead: on Stop / PreCompact / SessionEnd it runs `git stash create` (a dangling commit — working tree, index and branches untouched), pins it under refs/wilson-checkpoint/, and writes a resume note; SessionStart re-injects an unconsumed snapshot so the next session continues. Restore/list/clear via /wilson-checkpoint:checkpoint (restore is printed, never auto-applied). Debounced; git-only. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_CHECKPOINT=1.

plugins.name: wilson-gpu
plugins.description: Rented-GPU cost guardrail + lifecycle for RunPod / Vast.ai. SessionStart surfaces every still-billing instance (uptime + estimated cost so far) so a forgotten pod never leaks money; /wilson-gpu down is the kill switch and attach wires an instance into the wilson-pool roster. Operating strategies: watch (visibility), budget ($ ceiling escalation), idle-reaper (flag past max-hours), ephemeral (flag wilson-gpu-provisioned). Money/destroy are double-gated by SEPARATE off-by-default switches: provisioning (up also needs --yes) and reaping (any strategy auto-down). fanout is a cost-tolerance decision aid for shardable jobs (on/off, OFF by default — never auto-executes). Inert unless runpodctl / vastai is on PATH. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GPU=1.

plugins.name: wilson-decision-gate
plugins.description: Step-by-step decision gate — multi-decision work is one user-confirmation gate per decision, never batched: for each branch point present options + recommendation + rationale (3+ bullets), wait for the pick, then record a `### Decision N` block in design.md. Standalone port of wilson's principle-step-by-step-decision-gate (text-only, like wilson — a hook can't detect batching either). SessionStart **and PostCompact** inject the principle (PostCompact lands in the clean post-summary context — the rules survive compaction); UserPromptSubmit adds a short reminder ONLY on branch-point-looking prompts (not every prompt). /wilson-decision-gate maintains the design.md ledger; ships a 5-language canonical sample. Default ON; opt out: SIDECAR_NO_DECISION_GATE.

plugins.name: wilson-tape-recorder
plugins.description: Record the Claude Code session as a `.tape` v1.2 execution trace (the dancinlab tape format — github.com/dancinlab/tape). One file per session at <DATA>/sessions/<id>.tape: SessionStart writes `@S start`, UserPromptSubmit writes `@U`, PreToolUse writes `@T`, PostToolUse writes `@R`, SessionEnd writes `@S end`. Honest subset of the 17-type alphabet — only what CC hooks actually deliver (no @A assistant text, no @K cost — those signals aren't exposed). Pairs with `wilson-guards/tape-append-only` (the recorder PRODUCES `.tape`, the guard PROTECTS it). /wilson-tape-recorder for status / ls / tail / cat / on / off. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_TAPE_RECORDER=1.

plugins.name: wilson-goal
plugins.description: Session goal persistence + re-injection. Goal lives on disk at <DATA>/goal.json (outside the transcript that gets compacted), restored at every SessionStart **and PostCompact** (the clean post-summary moment) and re-asserted as a short reminder on each UserPromptSubmit. A project-local `GOAL.md` is used as the default when no user goal is set. /wilson-goal set|status|show|clear|path. HONEST GAP vs wilson's `loop` plugin: only the goal-persistence half is portable — the autonomous-continuation half (wilson's loop_tick + QUEUE auto-injection) cannot be done from CC hooks (no `continue without user input` mechanism). Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_GOAL=1.

plugins.name: wilson-resume
plugins.description: Resume continuity across an abrupt session end (usage limit / rate-limit / crash / closed terminal). wilson-checkpoint already restores the *files*; wilson-resume restores the *work narrative* — how far along the task was and what came next. Every Stop snapshots the model's own TodoWrite checklist (read from CC's on-disk ~/.claude/todos/<session>.json — N/M done, next pending), the last user request (transcript tail, bounded read) and the git working-tree state to a per-project file at <DATA>/<sha1(cwd)>.json. SessionEnd flips a clean-exit flag so the next session can tell a clean stop from an abrupt one. SessionStart (source startup/resume/clear) re-injects a short `## Resume` briefing; source=compact is skipped (PostCompact territory). Progress-only — the goal line stays wilson-goal's job, so the `## Resume` and `## Goal` blocks sit side by side without duplication. Capture is deterministic, no model-facing slash command. Companion to wilson-goal (what) and wilson-checkpoint (files). Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_RESUME=1.

plugins.name: wilson-inbox
plugins.description: Cross-project handoff inbox — when a gap or request affects another SSOT repo, file it there as a structured inbox/<kind>/<slug>.md entry (kind: notes / patches / poc / rfc_drafts) instead of silently working around it downstream. /wilson-inbox:inbox scaffolds and manages entries: add / list / show / path / verify / apply / archive / rm. Target repo: --to <name> (~/core/<name>) or the nearest .git walking up from the cwd. Light-mode is folder + entries; heavy-mode adds inbox/PATCHES.yaml with a status lifecycle that apply/archive transition. A SessionStart hook surfaces the current repo's inbox entries so a handoff is never forgotten — inert unless the repo has an inbox/. Standalone port of wilson's inbox plugin. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_INBOX=1.

plugins.name: wilson-lsp
plugins.description: Wires LSP servers for the dancinlab formats via .lsp.json: .hexa → `hexa lsp`; .tape / .n6 / .hxc / .kosmos → the canonical per-repo servers (tape-lsp / n6-lsp / hxc-lsp / kosmos-lsp, shipped in github.com/dancinlab/{tape,n6,hxc,kosmos}). Graceful: a server not on PATH just shows in /plugin Errors. Not a hook plugin — LSP lifecycle is CC-managed.

plugins.name: sidecar
plugins.description: Control plugin: /sidecar status|on|off <name> toggles the other sidecar plugins at runtime via a shared state file (~/.claude/sidecar/disabled.json) every plugin's hook checks. Persists across sessions; complements the native /plugin manager.

plugins.name: worktree-pr
plugins.description: Safe worktree -> PR -> merge -> cleanup workflow as the /worktree-pr:wt slash command. start makes an isolated worktree+branch off origin's default branch; ship pushes + opens a PR; finish merges the PR, removes the worktree, deletes the remote branch, refreshes the base. Never touches the main working tree or a concurrent session's branch.

plugins.name: wilson-sdlc
plugins.description: Spec → Plan → Build → Test&Recover → Review → Simplify → Ship — a 7-stage software-delivery skill pipeline. Skill *structure* is borrowed from addyosmani/agent-skills; the *content* is wilson's own dogfooded workflow, so each stage maps onto sidecar primitives the user already has: Spec→design.md+wilson-decision-gate, Plan→task tools+wilson-goal, Build→worktree-pr+wilson-checkpoint, Test&Recover→wilson-pool routing + systematic root-cause, Review→6-axis (5 generic + design.md decision-conformance), Simplify→sequences the built-in /simplify then re-checks decisions, Ship→the version→marketplace.json→GROWTH §10→commit checklist this very pack ships by. Stages that map to a wilson plugin degrade gracefully when that plugin is absent. Skills load on-demand; the plugin injects nothing on its own.
